breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 1.0 GENERAL
text: 1.0 GENERAL

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 1.0 GENERAL > Clause 1.1 This specification provides instructions for the laying of Fire Bricks in fired heaters, using
text:
1.1 This specification provides instructions for the laying of Fire Bricks in fired heaters, using
air setting mortar. It is also applicable for laying of bricks for firewalls & bridge walls in
fired heaters.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 1.0 GENERAL > Clause 1.2 The success of brick lining depends to a large extent on correct execution at site. It is
text:
1.2 The success of brick lining depends to a large extent on correct execution at site. It is
therefore imperative that the instructions provided herein are exactly followed. EIL
refractory specialist shall be consulted whenever it shall be considered requisite by the
Engineer-in-Charge.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 1.0 GENERAL > Clause 1.3 The lining work shall be carried out as per approved lining drawings and procedure by ELL
text:
1.3 The lining work shall be carried out as per approved lining drawings and procedure by ELL
/ PMC approved Agency.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 1.0 GENERAL > Clause 1.4 The Lining Agency shall obtain permission from EIC before start of lining.
text: 1.4 The Lining Agency shall obtain permission from EIC before start of lining.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 2.0 ANCHORING SYSTEM FOR BRICK WORK
text: 2.0 ANCHORING SYSTEM FOR BRICK WORK

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 2.0 ANCHORING SYSTEM FOR BRICK WORK > Clause 2.1 All brick linings on vertical flat casing shall be tied back to, and supported by the structural
text:
2.1 All brick linings on vertical flat casing shall be tied back to, and supported by the structural
steel framing members. Brick work may not require to be tied back in case the radius of
curvature of the wall is low enough to key-in the bricks. For flat walls, at least 20% of the
brick shall be tied back. Tie back shall extend into at least 1/3rd the thickness of the hot face
layer.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 2.0 ANCHORING SYSTEM FOR BRICK WORK > Clause 2.2 Sectional supports, if any, in the form of shelf flats / lintels shall be provided along the
text:
2.2 Sectional supports, if any, in the form of shelf flats / lintels shall be provided along the
height as per the arrangement drawing. The purpose of shelf flats is to reduce the cumulative
vertical expansion and to minimize maintenance in the event of localized repair.
Unless otherwise noted in arrangement drawing, MOC of shelf plate shall be SS310.
Support shelves shall be slotted to take care of differential thermal expansion. Support
shelves shall be regularly spaced vertically at approximate pitch of 1.8 meter. Vertical pitch
of support shelves shall be calculated based on load & thermal expansion and shall not
exceed 3.0 meter.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 2.0 ANCHORING SYSTEM FOR BRICK WORK > Clause 2.3 Horizontal Flats with holes for brick holding style shall be welded to the casing in each
text:
2.3 Horizontal Flats with holes for brick holding style shall be welded to the casing in each
panel as per the arrangement drawing. Material construction shall be as follows:
a) Flats:
Flats can be of CS material in case it is confined to the non-brick backup insulation layer
and the calculated design temperature is limited to 370 °C. However, as a minimum the
MOC indicated in the arrangement drawing shall be adopted.
b) Vertical Cleat Supports:
In case of vertical cleat supports where the cleat height is more than the depth of backup
insulation, the MOC of cleats shall be SS310 UON in the arrangement drawing.
2.4 The staples are normally made from 5mm dia. rods of SS310H to ASTM A479 Gr. 310H /
equivalent VON in arrangement drawing.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 3.0 PRELINING INSPECTION
text: 3.0 PRELINING INSPECTION

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 3.0 PRELINING INSPECTION > Clause 3.1 Prior to installation a thorough inspection shall be carried out for readiness for brick lining.
text: 3.1 Prior to installation a thorough inspection shall be carried out for readiness for brick lining.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 3.0 PRELINING INSPECTION
text:
ENGINEERS
zige_4116i5reg INDIA LIMITED INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING 6-17-0011 Rev. 2
1•402P1 IK,WIF ,RWA (4 ol Unoertaking, IN FIRED HEATERS

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 3.0 PRELINING INSPECTION > Clause 3.2 Check that the MOC and installation of all the items used in the anchoring system as defined
text:
3.2 Check that the MOC and installation of all the items used in the anchoring system as defined
in Clause 2.0 above are in accordance with the approved drawings. Horizontal flats with
holes or vertical cleats with slotted holes should be available on the casing for holding the
brick staples. Sectional supports in the form of shelf flats / lintels should be available for
supporting the base course of bricks.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 3.0 PRELINING INSPECTION > Clause 3.3 In case of brick lining with back-up layer, the back- up layer should have been installed in
text:
3.3 In case of brick lining with back-up layer, the back- up layer should have been installed in
position.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 3.0 PRELINING INSPECTION > Clause 3.4 There should be no undulations in the surface to be lined. The undulations can be the result
text:
3.4 There should be no undulations in the surface to be lined. The undulations can be the result
of distortion in the casing or local bulges due to poor quality of backup lining.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 3.0 PRELINING INSPECTION > Clause 3.5 The floor casing and the shelf flats which would seat the base course of bricks should also
text:
3.5 The floor casing and the shelf flats which would seat the base course of bricks should also
be checked for proper integrity, level and alignment.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 3.0 PRELINING INSPECTION > Clause 3.6 The condition and cleanliness of the surfaces to be lined shall be checked. The surfaces to
text:
3.6 The condition and cleanliness of the surfaces to be lined shall be checked. The surfaces to
come in contact with brick lining shall be cleaned to remove any dirt, grit, loose scale or
other foreign material.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 3.0 PRELINING INSPECTION > Clause 3.7 For equipment to be brick-lined, the temperature of the wet mix of mortar shall be
text:
3.7 For equipment to be brick-lined, the temperature of the wet mix of mortar shall be
maintained between 5-38°C. In case stringent requirement is specified by manufacturer, the
same shall be followed.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 3.0 PRELINING INSPECTION > Clause 3.8 In case of cylindrical surface 'Key' brick shall be inserted in full dimension, if required the
text:
3.8 In case of cylindrical surface 'Key' brick shall be inserted in full dimension, if required the
adjacent brick may be cut to suit (not less than the 50% of the width).

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 4.0 MATERIALS
text: 4.0 MATERIALS

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 4.0 MATERIALS > Clause 4.1 Materials shall be stored in original packaging as per supplier recommendations and shall
text:
4.1 Materials shall be stored in original packaging as per supplier recommendations and shall
be protected from moisture and foreign contaminants.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 4.0 MATERIALS > Clause 4.2 Bricks used shall be free from cracks, chips, broken edges / corners, spalling or other
text:
4.2 Bricks used shall be free from cracks, chips, broken edges / corners, spalling or other
defects. Bricks shall be evenly fired and of uniform color. Old bricks shall not be reclaimed
and used.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 4.0 MATERIALS > Clause 4.3 The mortar used for brick lining shall be non-slagging, air setting, chemically compatible
text:
4.3 The mortar used for brick lining shall be non-slagging, air setting, chemically compatible
with adjacent refractory and suitable for the rated temperature of brick.
Mortar used for jointing bridge wall bricks shall meet the following additional criteria:
I. Shall be suitable for continuous operating temperature of —1000°C min

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 4.0 MATERIALS > Clause 2 It should not contain any fluxing material like sodium silica
text:
2. It should not contain any fluxing material like sodium silica
Mortar used should be within the shelf life.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 4.0 MATERIALS > Clause 4.4 Air setting mortars are normally supplied in two forms:
text:
4.4 Air setting mortars are normally supplied in two forms:
a) Two Component System:
In the two-component system, powder is supplied in bags and liquid binders are
supplied in containers which are mixed at site prior to installation. This system is
normally preferred for bonding of IFBs.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 4.0 MATERIALS
text:
ilf-a.it4 STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR STANDARD SPECIFICATION No.
ks-- ENGINEERS
lfgar fEtit&g INDIA LIMITED INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING 6-17-0011 Rev. 2
ritant IA Goo of InOta Uncleftdiong, IN FIRED HEATERS

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 4.0 MATERIALS
text:
b) Single Component System:
This is supplied in powder form to which requisite amount of water is added as
recommended by the manufacturer. This system shall be used for bonding of IFBs only
with prior concurrence of EIL / Client / PMC.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 5.0 MIXING OF MORTAR
text: 5.0 MIXING OF MORTAR

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 5.0 MIXING OF MORTAR > Clause 5.1 Each component of the mortar shall he mixed in proper proportions as per instructions of
text:
5.1 Each component of the mortar shall he mixed in proper proportions as per instructions of
the manufacturer.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 5.0 MIXING OF MORTAR > Clause 5.2 Normally no conditioning or thinning of the mortar shall be carried out at site. In case same
text:
5.2 Normally no conditioning or thinning of the mortar shall be carried out at site. In case same
is required, it shall be carried out strictly as per the instructions of the manufacturer.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 5.0 MIXING OF MORTAR > Clause 5.3 Mortar shall preferably be mixed using a mechanical mortar mixer, unless otherwise
text:
5.3 Mortar shall preferably be mixed using a mechanical mortar mixer, unless otherwise
approved the EIC.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 5.0 MIXING OF MORTAR > Clause 5.4 Mixed mortar shall be consumed within half an hour of mixing. Normally 150 kg of mortar
text:
5.4 Mixed mortar shall be consumed within half an hour of mixing. Normally 150 kg of mortar
is required per 1000 nos. 230x115x75 size firebricks.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 5.0 MIXING OF MORTAR > Clause 5.5 If water is required to be used, same shall be of potable quality. The water temperature shall
text:
5.5 If water is required to be used, same shall be of potable quality. The water temperature shall
be between 15 to 30 deg C, UON by the manufacturer.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 5.0 MIXING OF MORTAR > Clause 5.6 Mortar shall be mixed to a uniform trowelling / dipping consistency as applicable for
text:
5.6 Mortar shall be mixed to a uniform trowelling / dipping consistency as applicable for
installation method.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING
text: 6.0 BRICK LAYING

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING > Clause 6.1 All brickwork shall be set out and built to the required dimensions as shown on the
text:
6.1 All brickwork shall be set out and built to the required dimensions as shown on the
arrangement drawing.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING > Clause 6.2 The base course of brick shall rest directly on the floor plate and not on another refractory.
text:
6.2 The base course of brick shall rest directly on the floor plate and not on another refractory.
The floor bricks shall not be mortared.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING > Clause 6.3 Anchored bricks shall be drilled by electric drills for making holes in to the brick. Proper
text:
6.3 Anchored bricks shall be drilled by electric drills for making holes in to the brick. Proper
care shall be paid to make the exact size of hole.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING > Clause 6.4 Tie back staples shall be provided as per drawing for anchoring the bricks. It shall be
text:
6.4 Tie back staples shall be provided as per drawing for anchoring the bricks. It shall be
checked that sufficient space is available for movement of the staple to accommodate the
vertical expansion of the brick wall.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING > Clause 6.5 All joints in the bricks shall be mortared on the sides and ends, except at expansion joint
text:
6.5 All joints in the bricks shall be mortared on the sides and ends, except at expansion joint
locations.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING > Clause 6.6 Mortar shall be smeared uniformly on the surface of the bricks with the help of trowels to
text:
6.6 Mortar shall be smeared uniformly on the surface of the bricks with the help of trowels to
provide smooth bedding of the bricks. The next brick should be dipped on one side and one
end and placed upon the brick which has been covered with mortar. This will prevent from
getting insufficient mortar between the joints.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING > Clause 6.7 Mallet (light wooden hammer) shall be used for compacting the bricks to keep it in position
text:
6.7 Mallet (light wooden hammer) shall be used for compacting the bricks to keep it in position
by removing the air voids and providing sound bonding.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING > Clause 6.8 The thickness of mortar joints shall be consistent, as thin as possible, and to be not more
text:
6.8 The thickness of mortar joints shall be consistent, as thin as possible, and to be not more
than 2mm.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING
text:

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING > Clause 6.9 Periodically, the verticality and straightness of walls shall be checked using spirit levels,
text:
6.9 Periodically, the verticality and straightness of walls shall be checked using spirit levels,
plumb, etc.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING > Clause 6.10 All walls shall be built up regularly, not leaving any part more than 1 meter lower than
text:
6.10 All walls shall be built up regularly, not leaving any part more than 1 meter lower than
another.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING > Clause 6.11 Any walls if, left at different levels shall be stepped and sufficiently supported to avoid
text:
6.11 Any walls if, left at different levels shall be stepped and sufficiently supported to avoid
sagging in any direction.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING > Clause 6.12 Any brick which gets cracked or broken during drilling or when anchor / staple is driven in,
text:
6.12 Any brick which gets cracked or broken during drilling or when anchor / staple is driven in,
must be rejected. •

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 6.0 BRICK LAYING > Clause 6.13 Fire bricks shall be saw cut to size. They shall not be "hammer" cut or "chipped" into places.
text: 6.13 Fire bricks shall be saw cut to size. They shall not be "hammer" cut or "chipped" into places.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 7.0 EXPANSION JOINTS
text: 7.0 EXPANSION JOINTS

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 7.0 EXPANSION JOINTS > Clause 7.1 Expansion joints shall be provided as given in the arrangement drawings. Width of the joints
text:
7.1 Expansion joints shall be provided as given in the arrangement drawings. Width of the joints
must not be less than the specified width.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 7.0 EXPANSION JOINTS > Clause 7.2 Expansion joints shall be provided in both vertical and horizontal directions of the walls, at
text:
7.2 Expansion joints shall be provided in both vertical and horizontal directions of the walls, at
wall edges and about burner tiles, doors and sleeved penetrations.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 7.0 EXPANSION JOINTS > Clause 7.3 Expansion joints shall be free of mortar.
text: 7.3 Expansion joints shall be free of mortar.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 7.0 EXPANSION JOINTS > Clause 7.4 Expansion joints shall be made by using plywood strips in the joints. Before insertion of
text:
7.4 Expansion joints shall be made by using plywood strips in the joints. Before insertion of
plywood strips, it shall be greased for its easy removal after the setting of brick lining.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 7.0 EXPANSION JOINTS > Clause 7.5 The expansion gap before filling shall be cleaned of any dust, debris or foreign material
text:
7.5 The expansion gap before filling shall be cleaned of any dust, debris or foreign material
before gap packing.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 7.0 EXPANSION JOINTS > Clause 7.6 Suitable packing material e.g. ceramic fiber of 128 kg/m3, I 425°C grade shall be used for
text:
7.6 Suitable packing material e.g. ceramic fiber of 128 kg/m3, I 425°C grade shall be used for
packing the expansion joints. The ceramic fiber shall be in blanket form and not loose
material. The ceramic fiber shall be attached to one side of the expansion joint by heat
resistant adhesive, to prevent dislodging during operation.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 7.0 EXPANSION JOINTS > Clause 7.7 If the bricks in a panel do not lay up to provide the width of expansion joints called for at
text:
7.7 If the bricks in a panel do not lay up to provide the width of expansion joints called for at
the top of the panel, the top course must be saw cut to obtain the correct width of expansion
joint.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 7.0 EXPANSION JOINTS > Clause 7.8 Top course of all bricks shall be cut back in slope to retain packing.
text: 7.8 Top course of all bricks shall be cut back in slope to retain packing.

breadcrumb: STANDARD SPECIFICATION FOR INSTALLATION OF BRICK LINING > 7.0 EXPANSION JOINTS > Clause 7.9 Special shaped bricks may be required as indicated on arrangement drawing. It is essential
text:
7.9 Special shaped bricks may be required as indicated on arrangement drawing. It is essential
that all mild steel parts shall be protected from furnace temperature and if any bricks require
to be cut on site this may be done quite easily with an ordinary saw.
